title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick — Page 279
raw_text:
[27:103] Below is a new model of my journey:
Starting at tilt point A (BIP 1974) I moved retrograde to 70 A.D., the actual
point of intervention, and then returned. Not to B, but to rectified point A, the
replacement world. For me to cross over from B to A, I had to move back to
X and then advance again along the replacement 4th axis track.
Perhaps the model should be drawn:
If this model is correct, I as "changeling" or "cuckoo's egg" floating on
the raft toward London, past Scotland Yard, am from another world, all right,
with [blocked] memories of it, but where that world is is that it's an alternate
world, not the far past (although I was briefly there), not the cyclum, not the
future, not another planet, etc.—any of these.
work_title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
